mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate exceeding 5.5% signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of instances where UNRATE > 5.5%, or t-stat for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real sales and housing starts indicate broad demand contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum during an economic expansion reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum fails to predict 5-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) during expansion phases, or returns are negative in >65% of positive-momentum observations.

mechanism_narrative: Low nominal interest rates combined with subdued monthly inflation indicate accommodative real policy conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in >65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding labor demand and rising household income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 2.0% signals demand stagnation and weak corporate earnings momentum, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >70% of low-GDP-growth observations, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and slowing industrial production indicates supply-side fragility and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition coincides with positive 20-day returns in >65% of cases, or the interaction term fails to improve predictive power over single-series models.
